title: Pseudo file tree component with search and navigation
purpose: Provides a file tree UI for pseudo files with search filtering, collapse/expand functionality, and navigation capabilities.
module_context: Exports TreeNode type and TreeNodeRenderer component for reuse. Defines PseudoFileTree component that manages search input and delegates rendering to PseudoTreeBody.

steps.content: Render a chevron SVG icon that rotates based on the isDown prop.
Apply CSS styles to rotate the icon and add a smooth transition effect.

methods.name: TreeNodeRenderer
methods.normalized_params: (node: TreeNode, level: number, currentPath: string, collapsedDirs: Set<string>, onToggleCollapse: (path: string) => void, onNavigate: (path: string) => void, filterExpanded: Set<string>, fileMeta: Map<string, PseudoFileSummary>): JSX.Element

steps.content: Determine if the current node is collapsed, active, or has children.
Calculate whether to show children based on collapse state and filter expansion.
Retrieve session and tab store data for linking files to sessions.
Define a handler to link a file to the current session and open it as a tab.
Render the node with appropriate styling and conditional elements based on node type.
Render child nodes recursively if they should be shown.

methods.name: PseudoFileTree
methods.normalized_params: (fileList: PseudoFileSummary[], currentPath: string, onNavigate: (stem: string) => void, project: string): JSX.Element

steps.content: Extract the search query and setter from the sidebar tree store.
Perform a one-time migration of legacy collapsed paths from local storage to the shared store.
IF the file list is empty, render a message indicating no files are available.
Render the search input field with appropriate styling and event handlers.
Delegate rendering of the file tree body to the PseudoTreeBody component.
Check if a project is defined before attempting migration.
Load legacy collapsed paths from local storage using a project-specific key.
IF legacy data exists, parse it and validate its structure.
Merge legacy paths with existing collapsed paths in the store.
Update the store with the merged collapsed paths and remove the legacy data from local storage.
